turns.svg:
<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="512" height="512" viewBox="0 0 512 512">
  <defs>
    <linearGradient id="bg" x1="0" y1="0" x2="0" y2="1">
      <stop offset="0" stop-color="#f8fbff" />
      <stop offset="1" stop-color="#eef3f8" />
    </linearGradient>
    <linearGradient id="light" x1="0" y1="0" x2="0" y2="1">
      <stop offset="0" stop-color="#fff9c4" stop-opacity="0.9" />
      <stop offset="1" stop-color="#fff59d" stop-opacity="0.0" />
    </linearGradient>
  </defs>

  <!-- background -->
  <rect width="512" height="512" fill="url(#bg)" />

  <!-- desk surface -->
  <rect x="0" y="380" width="512" height="132" fill="#d9b88a" />
  <rect x="0" y="368" width="512" height="12" fill="#c89e6b" />

  <!-- stack of books left -->
  <g transform="translate(48,300)">
    <rect x="0" y="44" width="120" height="22" rx="3" fill="#8fbcd4" stroke="#6fa3bc" />
    <rect x="6" y="20" width="108" height="22" rx="3" fill="#f1a66a" stroke="#d4884f" />
    <rect x="12" y="-4" width="96" height="22" rx="3" fill="#b2d7a6" stroke="#93bb86" />
  </g>

  <!-- pencil cup -->
  <g transform="translate(156,320)">
    <ellipse cx="0" cy="36" rx="24" ry="8" fill="#c76a6a" opacity="0.95" />
    <rect x="-24" y="-8" width="48" height="44" rx="6" fill="#e07b7b" />
    <!-- pencils -->
    <g>
      <rect x="-8" y="-30" width="10" height="38" fill="#f4d35e" transform="rotate(-8 -8 -30)" />
      <polygon points="2,-32 6,-28 2,-26" fill="#e07b7b" transform="rotate(-8 -8 -30)" />
      <rect x="6" y="-36" width="10" height="44" fill="#8ac6d1" transform="rotate(-18 6 -36)" />
      <polygon points="14,-38 18,-34 14,-30" fill="#3f7a9e" transform="rotate(-18 6 -36)" />
      <rect x="-20" y="-34" width="10" height="40" fill="#b084d7" transform="rotate(12 -20 -34)" />
      <polygon points="-12,-36 -8,-32 -12,-28" fill="#6b3fa0" transform="rotate(12 -20 -34)" />
    </g>
  </g>

  <!-- open book center -->
  <g transform="translate(160,260)">
    <!-- left page -->
    <path d="M0,40 C20,20 60,12 110,30 L110,80 C70,72 30,64 0,60 Z" fill="#ffffff" stroke="#d7d7d7" stroke-width="2" />
    <!-- right page -->
    <path d="M110,30 C160,12 200,20 220,40 C220,60 180,68 140,72 L110,80 Z" fill="#ffffff" stroke="#d7d7d7" stroke-width="2" />
    <!-- crease -->
    <path d="M110,30 L110,80" stroke="#c0c0c0" stroke-width="1.5" />
    <!-- faint lines on pages -->
    <g stroke="#d8d8d8" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-width="2">
      <path d="M18,48 L92,48" />
      <path d="M18,56 L92,56" />
      <path d="M18,64 L92,64" />
      <path d="M128,48 L204,48" />
      <path d="M128,56 L204,56" />
      <path d="M128,64 L204,64" />
    </g>
  </g>

  <!-- glasses on book -->
  <g transform="translate(210,310)">
    <ellipse cx="-20" cy="-6" rx="18" ry="12" fill="none" stroke="#5b5b5b" stroke-width="4" />
    <ellipse cx="42" cy="-6" rx="18" ry="12" fill="none" stroke="#5b5b5b" stroke-width="4" />
    <path d="M-2,-6 Q10,-6 20,-6" stroke="#5b5b5b" stroke-width="4" fill="none" />
    <path d="M-38,-6 Q-50,-4 -54,2" stroke="#5b5b5b" stroke-width="4" fill="none" />
    <path d="M80,-6 Q92,-4 96,2" stroke="#5b5b5b" stroke-width="4" fill="none" />
  </g>

  <!-- desk lamp right -->
  <g transform="translate(380,210)">
    <!-- light cone -->
    <path d="M0,20 L60,140 L-60,140 Z" fill="url(#light)" opacity="0.95" />
    <!-- shade -->
    <path d="M0,-6 C32,-6 52,8 60,26 C42,20 22,22 0,22 C-22,22 -42,20 -60,26 C-52,8 -32,-6 0,-6 Z" fill="#f6d166" stroke="#cca84a" stroke-width="2" />
    <!-- stem -->
    <rect x="-6" y="22" width="12" height="70" rx="6" fill="#bdbdbd" />
    <rect x="-4" y="90" width="8" height="8" rx="2" fill="#9e9e9e" />
    <!-- base -->
    <ellipse cx="0" cy="110" rx="28" ry="10" fill="#7f7f7f" />
  </g>

</svg>

messages.content:
You are a drawing agent in a one-word guessing game.
Return JSON only, with a single field: svg.

Rules:
1) Draw the target word using only SVG graphics.
2) Output one complete <svg>...</svg> string.
3) Do not include text labels, letters, or numbers in the drawing.
4) Keep the SVG simple and valid. No scripts, no foreignObject.
5) Use a 512x512 canvas.

Output schema:
{ "svg": "string" }

message.content:
{
  "svg": "<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="512" height="512" viewBox="0 0 512 512">
  <defs>
    <linearGradient id="bg" x1="0" y1="0" x2="0" y2="1">
      <stop offset="0" stop-color="#f8fbff" />
      <stop offset="1" stop-color="#eef3f8" />
    </linearGradient>
    <linearGradient id="light" x1="0" y1="0" x2="0" y2="1">
      <stop offset="0" stop-color="#fff9c4" stop-opacity="0.9" />
      <stop offset="1" stop-color="#fff59d" stop-opacity="0.0" />
    </linearGradient>
  </defs>

  <!-- background -->
  <rect width="512" height="512" fill="url(#bg)" />

  <!-- desk surface -->
  <rect x="0" y="380" width="512" height="132" fill="#d9b88a" />
  <rect x="0" y="368" width="512" height="12" fill="#c89e6b" />

  <!-- stack of books left -->
  <g transform="translate(48,300)">
    <rect x="0" y="44" width="120" height="22" rx="3" fill="#8fbcd4" stroke="#6fa3bc" />
    <rect x="6" y="20" width="108" height="22" rx="3" fill="#f1a66a" stroke="#d4884f" />
    <rect x="12" y="-4" width="96" height="22" rx="3" fill="#b2d7a6" stroke="#93bb86" />
  </g>

  <!-- pencil cup -->
  <g transform="translate(156,320)">
    <ellipse cx="0" cy="36" rx="24" ry="8" fill="#c76a6a" opacity="0.95" />
    <rect x="-24" y="-8" width="48" height="44" rx="6" fill="#e07b7b" />
    <!-- pencils -->
    <g>
      <rect x="-8" y="-30" width="10" height="38" fill="#f4d35e" transform="rotate(-8 -8 -30)" />
      <polygon points="2,-32 6,-28 2,-26" fill="#e07b7b" transform="rotate(-8 -8 -30)" />
      <rect x="6" y="-36" width="10" height="44" fill="#8ac6d1" transform="rotate(-18 6 -36)" />
      <polygon points="14,-38 18,-34 14,-30" fill="#3f7a9e" transform="rotate(-18 6 -36)" />
      <rect x="-20" y="-34" width="10" height="40" fill="#b084d7" transform="rotate(12 -20 -34)" />
      <polygon points="-12,-36 -8,-32 -12,-28" fill="#6b3fa0" transform="rotate(12 -20 -34)" />
    </g>
  </g>

  <!-- open book center -->
  <g transform="translate(160,260)">
    <!-- left page -->
    <path d="M0,40 C20,20 60,12 110,30 L110,80 C70,72 30,64 0,60 Z" fill="#ffffff" stroke="#d7d7d7" stroke-width="2" />
    <!-- right page -->
    <path d="M110,30 C160,12 200,20 220,40 C220,60 180,68 140,72 L110,80 Z" fill="#ffffff" stroke="#d7d7d7" stroke-width="2" />
    <!-- crease -->
    <path d="M110,30 L110,80" stroke="#c0c0c0" stroke-width="1.5" />
    <!-- faint lines on pages -->
    <g stroke="#d8d8d8" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-width="2">
      <path d="M18,48 L92,48" />
      <path d="M18,56 L92,56" />
      <path d="M18,64 L92,64" />
      <path d="M128,48 L204,48" />
      <path d="M128,56 L204,56" />
      <path d="M128,64 L204,64" />
    </g>
  </g>

  <!-- glasses on book -->
  <g transform="translate(210,310)">
    <ellipse cx="-20" cy="-6" rx="18" ry="12" fill="none" stroke="#5b5b5b" stroke-width="4" />
    <ellipse cx="42" cy="-6" rx="18" ry="12" fill="none" stroke="#5b5b5b" stroke-width="4" />
    <path d="M-2,-6 Q10,-6 20,-6" stroke="#5b5b5b" stroke-width="4" fill="none" />
    <path d="M-38,-6 Q-50,-4 -54,2" stroke="#5b5b5b" stroke-width="4" fill="none" />
    <path d="M80,-6 Q92,-4 96,2" stroke="#5b5b5b" stroke-width="4" fill="none" />
  </g>

  <!-- desk lamp right -->
  <g transform="translate(380,210)">
    <!-- light cone -->
    <path d="M0,20 L60,140 L-60,140 Z" fill="url(#light)" opacity="0.95" />
    <!-- shade -->
    <path d="M0,-6 C32,-6 52,8 60,26 C42,20 22,22 0,22 C-22,22 -42,20 -60,26 C-52,8 -32,-6 0,-6 Z" fill="#f6d166" stroke="#cca84a" stroke-width="2" />
    <!-- stem -->
    <rect x="-6" y="22" width="12" height="70" rx="6" fill="#bdbdbd" />
    <rect x="-4" y="90" width="8" height="8" rx="2" fill="#9e9e9e" />
    <!-- base -->
    <ellipse cx="0" cy="110" rx="28" ry="10" fill="#7f7f7f" />
  </g>

</svg>"
}
message.reasoning:
**Creating SVG for 'study'**

I need to create an SVG image representing "study" without any text. The imagery should include symbolic elements like an open book, desk lamp, a cup with pencils, glasses, a notepad, and a stack of books. I have to ensure it's a simple SVG string within a 512x512 canvas and avoid using scripts or foreignObject. It’s important to return valid JSON, escaping quotes properly, so the structure looks like: { "svg": "<svg ...>...</svg>" } with the right color scheme and minimal details.**Designing SVG Elements for 'Study'**

I’m creating an SVG image to represent "study" without any text. The desk will be a rectangle at the bottom, and I’ll position it at a specific height. An open book needs to have two polygon-shaped pages with soft curves and light strokes for detail, plus thin gray lines to imply text without using letters. I'll also draw a lamp with circular and conical shapes, a pencil cup, and glasses using simple shapes with fill colors. The SVG will be 512x512 pixels, with a subtle background color.**Creating SVG Components for 'Study'**

I’m working on adding more components to the SVG for "study." The lamp will have a circular base, a stem made from a path, and a yellowish shade with a gradient or translucent effect. On the left side, I’ll draw a cylinder for the pencil cup, filled with rectangle-shaped colored pencils that have triangular tips. There will also be a stack of books represented by rectangles with different fills, and glasses resting on the book with two ellipses and a bridge. I have to ensure there are no ID conflicts and properly escape the quotes for the JSON output.

messages.content:
You are the guesser in a visual one-word guessing game.
Return JSON only, with a single field: guesses.

Rules:
1) You receive one JPEG image of a drawing.
2) Return exactly 20 one-word guesses in order of confidence (best guess first).
3) The order is important and will be scored by first correct position.
4) Do not repeat guesses.
5) Each guess must be EN-US one word, no spaces.
6) Avoid punctuation and numbers.

Output schema:
{ "guesses": ["string", "... exactly 20 items ..."] }

message.reasoning:
**Generating one-word guesses**

I need to output JSON with exactly 20 one-word guesses based on the image description. The drawing is simple, showing elements like a stack of books, a pen holder, an open book with glasses, and a desk lamp. 

My top guesses might include "reading," "book," and "study." I’ll aim to arrange these guesses in order of confidence, avoiding punctuation and spaces. I wonder if “reading” is the best word to start with, but I'll also consider other synonyms related to these themes.**Constructing word guesses**

I see that the central object is an open book accompanied by glasses and a lamp, which leads me to think of guesses like "reading" or "book." While "book" seems straightforward, "reading" might be the intended focus. I think "book" is a safer first choice. 

I need to compile 20 one-word guesses in descending order of confidence, using terms related to the scene, ensuring they are lowercase with no spaces or punctuation. I’ll include words like "openbook," "bookworm," and "spectacles," but avoid plural forms.**Compiling guesses in JSON format**

I’m crafting a list of one-word guesses based on the scene. My ordered list includes words like "book," "reading," and "study." I've confirmed that each entry is one word without spaces. Words like "openbook," "bookworm," and "workspace" are acceptable. 

I’ll make sure there are no duplicates and count to ensure there are exactly 20 terms. Once confirmed, I’ll format the output as exact JSON, ensuring everything is in lowercase. Alright, it’s time to produce that JSON!
